authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 11870 - Customer Account Transfer Contracts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 11870 is the primary broker-dealer rulebook source for customer account transfer timing, responsibilities, and exception handling across receiving and delivering firms.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains ACATS and transfer workflow states, missing-prerequisite handling, and refusal of unsupported overrides.

authoritative_sources.title: Automated Customer Account Transfer Service (ACATS)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC operates the market infrastructure used for ACATS processing and is the authoritative public workflow source for transfer statuses, records, and message handling.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines realistic ACATS terminology, execution-state wording, and exception-note structures for brokerage transfer processing.

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Alert: Division of Examinations T+1 Settlement Cycle
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC examinations guidance is an authoritative public source for operational controls around allocations, confirmations, affirmations, and the shortened settlement cycle.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains settlement blocker handling, completion evidence, and escalation for late or failed settlement states.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 2232 - Customer Confirmations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 2232 is an authoritative public broker-dealer rule for what operational evidence must support customer trade confirmations and related disclosures.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains confirmation-complete claims and requires brokerage outputs to distinguish sent confirmation evidence from internal trade status alone.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 4511 - General Requirements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 4511 is a core books-and-records control baseline that governs the preservation and availability of records supporting broker-dealer operational activity and review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Record truth and completion evidence review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines what evidence must exist before a completion record or operational summary can be treated as audit-ready.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 4512 - Customer Account Information
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 4512 is the public rulebook baseline for the account-information and trusted-contact record set that establishes customer and account truth for brokerage operations.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains what minimum account records must exist before the lane can progress a transfer, cashiering change, or client-impacting action.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 3310 - Anti-Money Laundering Compliance Program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 3310 is the core broker-dealer AML rulebook baseline and is authoritative for escalation posture when suspicious activity, monitoring alerts, or CIP dependencies surface in brokerage operations.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains brokerage outputs so suspicious activity is routed to AML owners instead of being resolved as routine processing.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Regulatory Notice 23-06 - Risks Related to ACATS Fraud and Registered Representative Misconduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Regulatory Notice 23-06 is authoritative public guidance for ACATS-related fraud indicators, account-takeover risks, and mitigation practices relevant to brokerage operations routing and escalation.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains transfer-related fraud scenarios so the lane escalates suspicious transfer behavior instead of narrating it away as routine operations.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate account identity, customer authorization, restrictions, callback state, disclosure context, and tenant-specific approvals before any brokerage-operations work progresses.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Account, transfer, and cashiering instruction processing
workflow_stages.description: Move account-maintenance, transfer, and money-movement requests through request, review, hold, or reject states using named workflow records and system messages.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Trade confirmation, settlement, and reconciliation exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Track confirmation obligations, settlement blockers, returned funds, and reconciliation breaks without claiming that unresolved discrepancies are closed.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence capture and audit-ready closure
workflow_stages.description: Produce completion or fulfillment records only when the underlying books, ledger entries, transfer statuses, confirmation records, and customer communications support closure.

workflow_stages.name: Fraud, AML, and client-protection escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate suspicious transfer behavior, account-takeover indicators, and client-harm risk rather than allowing operational speed to override controls.

artifacts: Account master, registration, entitlement, trusted-contact, standing-instruction, and restriction records
Transfer forms, ACATS records, cashiering instructions, callback logs, and approval-history artifacts
Trade tickets, cancel and correct records, confirmation records, disclosure delivery logs, and settlement-status messages
Position, custody, suspense, general-ledger tie-out, and reconciliation exception logs
KYC, AML, fraud, dispute, and customer communication artifacts
Case-management records and document images tied to the brokerage workflow
systems_of_record: Broker-dealer account master and books-and-records systems
Order management, trade-support, clearance, and settlement systems
CRM, case-management, and document management systems
Fraud monitoring, AML case-management, and compliance escalation systems

allowed: Determine whether available records support progression, hold, rework, or completion within brokerage-operations execution workflows.
Produce execution summaries, completion evidence, exception notes, and escalation packages tied to named account, trade, transfer, confirmation, and communication records.
Identify when transfer, cashiering, settlement, reconciliation, or confirmation blockers require the next operational owner.
escalate: A fraud, AML, account-takeover, or client-impact indicator changes the risk posture.
A legal, disclosure, or policy interpretation materially changes whether the request may proceed.
Account, confirmation, ledger, transfer, or reconciliation records conflict and need human review or adjacent-owner resolution.
refuse: The request asks for investment advice, suitability, underwriting, legal interpretation, or final compliance signoff outside the lane.
The request asks to approve a disbursement, bypass a hold, override callback controls, or mark work complete without authoritative evidence.
Required tenant facts, customer authorization, or system-of-record context are missing and would change the outcome.

domain_failure_modes: Advancing a transfer or money movement without documented customer authorization, callback evidence, or required account restrictions review.
Claiming completion while confirmation, settlement, cashiering, or ledger records still show unresolved breaks or open exceptions.
Treating ACATS, cashiering, and internal tracker statuses as equivalent when they conflict materially.
Allowing urgency or client pressure to override documented holds, approval thresholds, disclosure requirements, or callback controls.
Missing account-takeover, suspicious-activity, or client-harm indicators embedded in transfer, cashiering, or account-change behavior.
Collapsing operational handling into investment advice, fraud adjudication, underwriting, or legal interpretation.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific temporary-hold, elder-abuse, and customer-notice requirements beyond the FINRA brokerage baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline supports fraud and client-protection escalation, but tenant jurisdiction may impose additional notice, reporting, or hold-duration rules that must be supplied at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Product-specific margin, option, retirement, and alternative-asset constraints on transferability and money movement
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack assumes the public brokerage baseline; product-specific transferability, collateral, and approval rules must come from tenant retrieval and product policy overlays.

notes: The public baseline is sufficient for a validated brokerage-operations spec pack because it covers workflow, evidence, terminology, account truth, and failure handling, while explicitly leaving tenant-specific products, approval maps, and jurisdictional overlays to runtime retrieval.
The lane is intentionally limited to operational handling and does not claim advisory, legal, fraud-adjudication, underwriting, or final compliance authority.
